turns.content:
# Session-API Line-Count Audit Handoff

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend

- `AUDIT-TICKET` = [d8cb1b87 [session-api][audit] Remediate default file-length findings](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/d8cb1b87-48a2-4a99-b741-48cfaed44711/ticket.toml)
- `HANDOFF` = persisted handoff `e579cb4f-39ce-4137-b744-e932fa919546`
- `SESSION` = durable workspace session `03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f`
- `RUN` = outgoing session run `52e1cdc9-d4b9-4c8d-8316-615151b909c8`

Resume the remaining `session-api` file-length remediation from `memory-api/`. `AUDIT-TICKET` is already `in-implementation` and has no active board ownership. The most recent captured audit selector used a 1,000-line threshold and identified two remaining violations; the ticket’s actual completion gate is stricter: the unparameterized default audit uses `max_file_lines = 400`.

## Durable Session Identity

- `workspace_session_id`: `03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f`
- outgoing `run_id`: `52e1cdc9-d4b9-4c8d-8316-615151b909c8`
- `handoff_id`: `e579cb4f-39ce-4137-b744-e932fa919546`
- Exact resume command:
  `session-cli resume --workspace-session-id 03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f --predecessor-run-id 52e1cdc9-d4b9-4c8d-8316-615151b909c8`
- Persisted handoff artifact: [e579cb4f-39ce-4137-b744-e932fa919546.json](.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs/e579cb4f-39ce-4137-b744-e932fa919546.json)

## Key Entities

- `AUDIT-TICKET`: [d8cb1b87 [session-api][audit] Remediate default file-length findings](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/d8cb1b87-48a2-4a99-b741-48cfaed44711/ticket.toml) is the implementation tracker and defines the default-400-line acceptance gate.
- [AUDIT-TICKET scope and validation contract](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/d8cb1b87-48a2-4a99-b741-48cfaed44711/description.md) explains the historical threshold confusion and records the required validation commands.
- [hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs) is 1,034 lines under the 1,000-line audit and contains transcript parsing, event-envelope construction, event dispatch, and capture-record conversion.
- [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs) is 1,156 lines under the 1,000-line audit and contains explicit-ingestion extraction, failed-tool mapping, feedback-entry recovery, and unit tests.
- [hook tests](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs) exercise the hook parser and are the immediate behavioral regression surface.
- [feedback-ingestion ticket](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4954d6c-0451-49ed-8939-11f6568558f5/ticket.toml) documents the prior event-based ingestion behavior and focused `transcript_feedback` validation; preserve that public behavior while splitting modules.
- [hook-capture ticket](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b3155a94-230e-416b-be0e-5948d6d2193a/ticket.toml) records passing hook E2E coverage and is useful context for externally visible capture behavior.

## Findings And Decisions

- The prior captured audit report, filtered to `crates/session-api/` with `line_count > 1000`, reported:
  - [hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs): 1,034 lines, threshold 1,000.
  - [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs): 1,156 lines, threshold 1,000.
- `AUDIT-TICKET`’s description is stale for `hook.rs`: it says 726 lines. Update its implementation/validation summary with the actual post-refactor audit result rather than treating that historical list as current.
- Do not use the 1,000-line selector as the definition of done. The ticket explicitly corrects the rule: run the canonical, unparameterized audit against `memory-api`; no `session-api` file may remain above the default 400-line limit.
- Prefer extraction over behavioral rewrites:
  - [hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs) already has a `tool_execution` child module. Likely extractions are event-envelope types and parsing/normalization helpers into cohesive private submodules while preserving the existing public re-exports from `session_api`.
  - [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs) already separates `entity_discovery`, `event_outcomes`, and `turn_signals`; extract the large failed-tool mapping policy and/or recovery logic next, preserving public API locations through re-exports as needed.
- The baseline compile check completed successfully:
  `rtk cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/crates/session-api/Cargo.toml --no-run`.
- Rendering the root durable session’s pinned instructions is currently blocked by stale pin `ce://default/rule/84fa9769-cff9-4d89-9068-88474584b4b3`, which no longer resolves. This does not block the Rust refactor; resume the session and either repair/remove that pin through the appropriate rule/session workflow or record it as a separate bootstrap defect.

## Board And Worktree Notes

- The root board has one stale entry for unrelated feedback-MCP work; it owns only `memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/**` and an unrelated spec. It does not conflict with `session-api`.
- `memory-api` was clean on branch `main` when this handoff was created.
- The root worktree has unrelated modifications, including `.gitignore`, a root spec body, the `memory-api` submodule pointer, and untracked `.session/sessions/**`. Do not revert, stage, or modify them for this audit track.
- Before editing, check out `AUDIT-TICKET` on the memory-api board and claim only the source/test files actually selected for the first split.

## Next Actions

1. Resume `SESSION` using the exact command above, `cd memory-api`, inspect `AUDIT-TICKET`, then check it out and claim the first selected file set.
2. Run the canonical audit against `memory-api` with no `max_file_lines` override. This is the first discriminating check: it establishes the live default-400 queue and confirms whether the two 1,000-line findings are still the only remaining work in this track.
3. Read the local portions of [hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs) and [hook tests](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs), choose one cohesive extraction that reduces the file materially without changing public API, and make that smallest split.
4. Immediately run `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib`. Repair only failures attributable to that extraction and rerun the same command.
5. Repeat for [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs), retaining event-based mining, exact mapping policy, and recovery semantics. Run the focused relevant test selector first when practical, then `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib`.
6. Rerun the canonical unparameterized audit. Continue splitting any `session-api` file still over 400 lines until the audit has no `session-api` `line_count` findings.
7. Update `AUDIT-TICKET` with final source-file list, exact audit evidence, test result, any remaining blocker, and move it to `in-review` only after the required gate passes.

Execute these actions in order and continue until every action is completed or explicitly blocked.

## Completion Status

- Resume durable session: `not-started` - exact lineage and handoff record are available.
- Check out and claim `AUDIT-TICKET`: `not-started` - no relevant active ownership exists.
- Establish live default-audit queue: `not-started` - prior evidence includes a custom 1,000-line selector, while acceptance is 400 lines.
- Split and validate `hook.rs`: `not-started` - current measured size is 1,034 lines.
- Split and validate `transcript_feedback.rs`: `not-started` - current measured size is 1,156 lines.
- Canonical audit closure and ticket update: `not-started` - required after all splits.
- Stale durable-rule pin repair: `blocked` - the unresolved rule pin prevents focused instruction rendering but is not a blocker for the audit implementation.

## Definition Of Done

`AUDIT-TICKET` is ready for review when `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib` passes after each implementation slice, the canonical unparameterized audit against `memory-api` reports no `session-api` `line_count` finding above 400 lines, public behavior/re-exports remain compatible, and the ticket contains the final audit and validation evidence.
I’ll resume the supplied session lineage, establish the live default audit queue, then claim and split the smallest cohesive `session-api` slice before running its focused library tests.

arguments.query: Resume a durable workspace session, render its pinned instructions, and view the current runtime session state.

turns.content: I’ve loaded the session, ticket-board, and audit interfaces. I’m resuming the named workspace while checking the board’s current ownership; the default audit then decides which file to claim first.

The board confirms the only active entry owns unrelated feedback-MCP files. The audit completed but returned a large report; I’m narrowing it to file-level findings and retrying the session resume with the explicit workspace path required by the runtime.

The canonical default audit is live and shows that the scope is broader than the prior 1,000-line snapshot: `hook.rs`, `peek.rs`, and the capture-hook binary each have findings, alongside the original `transcript_feedback.rs`. I’ll inspect the tracker and the exact `session-api` line-count list, then claim only the hook surface for the first extraction.

*** Update File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\memory-api\crates\session-api\src\peek.rs

 pub use prompt_pack::{
@@
     peek_prompt_pack,
 };
+pub use views::{
+    SessionSkeleton,
+    SessionSkeletonEntry,
+    SessionTurnRange,
+    peek_skeleton,
+    peek_turn_range,
+};
+
+mod views;
 
-/// A bounded window of transcript turns for a single session.
-///
-/// This is a read-side view transform over an already-persisted
-/// [`SessionRecord`]; it does not mutate or re-persist any session state.
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
-pub struct SessionTurnRange {
-    pub session_id: String,
-    pub total_turns: usize,
-    /// Inclusive start index (0-based) of the returned window.
-    pub start: usize,
-    /// Exclusive end index (0-based) of the returned window.
-    pub end: usize,
-    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
-    pub turns: Vec<SessionTurn>,
-}
-
-/// A single compact entry in a [`SessionSkeleton`].
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
-pub struct SessionSkeletonEntry {
-    pub sequence: usize,
-    pub role: SessionRole,
-    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub tool_name: Option<String>,
-    /// Truncated single-line preview of the turn content.
-    pub preview: String,
-    /// Full character length of the original turn content.
-    pub content_len: usize,
-}
-
-/// A compact, body-stripped overview of a session transcript.
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
-pub struct SessionSkeleton {
-    pub session_id: String,
-    pub total_turns: usize,
-    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
-    pub entries: Vec<SessionSkeletonEntry>,
-}
-
-/// Return a bounded window of turns from `record`.
-///
-/// `start` is clamped to `[0, total]`. `end` defaults to `total` and is clamped
-/// to `[start, total]`, so callers always receive a valid, non-panicking slice.
-pub fn peek_turn_range(
-    record: &SessionRecord,
-    start: usize,
-    end: Option<usize>,
-) -> SessionTurnRange {
-    let total = record.turns.len();
-    let start = start.min(total);
-    let end = end.unwrap_or(total).clamp(start, total);
-
-    SessionTurnRange {
-        session_id: record.session_id.clone(),
-        total_turns: total,
-        start,
-        end,
-        turns: record.turns[start..end].to_vec(),
-    }
-}
-
-/// Return a compact skeleton of `record`, stripping turn bodies to a single
-/// truncated preview line each.
-///
-/// `preview_chars` bounds the preview length; a value of `0` yields empty
-/// previews while still reporting `content_len`.
-pub fn peek_skeleton(
-    record: &SessionRecord,
-    preview_chars: usize,
-) -> SessionSkeleton {
-    let entries = record
-        .turns
-        .iter()
-        .map(|turn| SessionSkeletonEntry {
-            sequence: turn.sequence,
-            role: turn.role.clone(),
-            tool_name: turn.tool_name.clone(),
-            preview: preview_line(&turn.content, preview_chars),
-            content_len: turn.content.chars().count(),
-        })
-        .collect();
-
-    SessionSkeleton {
-        session_id: record.session_id.clone(),
-        total_turns: record.turns.len(),
-        entries,
-    }
-}
-
 /// Build a single-line, character-bounded preview of `content`.
@@
 mod tests {
*** Add File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\memory-api\crates\session-api\src\peek\views.rs
+use serde::{
+    Deserialize,
+    Serialize,
+};
+
+use crate::{
+    SessionRecord,
+    SessionRole,
+    SessionTurn,
+};
+
+use super::preview_line;
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionTurnRange {
+    pub session_id: String,
+    pub total_turns: usize,
+    pub start: usize,
+    pub end: usize,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub turns: Vec<SessionTurn>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionSkeletonEntry {
+    pub sequence: usize,
+    pub role: SessionRole,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub tool_name: Option<String>,
+    pub preview: String,
+    pub content_len: usize,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionSkeleton {
+    pub session_id: String,
+    pub total_turns: usize,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub entries: Vec<SessionSkeletonEntry>,
+}
+
+pub fn peek_turn_range(
+    record: &SessionRecord,
+    start: usize,
+    end: Option<usize>,
+) -> SessionTurnRange {
+    let total = record.turns.len();
+    let start = start.min(total);
+    let end = end.unwrap_or(total).clamp(start, total);
+
+    SessionTurnRange {
+        session_id: record.session_id.clone(),
+        total_turns: total,
+        start,
+        end,
+        turns: record.turns[start..end].to_vec(),
+    }
+}
+
+pub fn peek_skeleton(
+    record: &SessionRecord,
+    preview_chars: usize,
+) -> SessionSkeleton {
+    let entries = record
+        .turns
+        .iter()
+        .map(|turn| SessionSkeletonEntry {
+            sequence: turn.sequence,
+            role: turn.role.clone(),
+            tool_name: turn.tool_name.clone(),
+            preview: preview_line(&turn.content, preview_chars),
+            content_len: turn.content.chars().count(),
+        })
+        .collect();
+
+    SessionSkeleton {
+        session_id: record.session_id.clone(),
+        total_turns: record.turns.len(),
+        entries,
+    }
+}
*** End Patch

+use chrono::{
+    DateTime,
+    Utc,
+};
+use serde::{
+    Deserialize,
+    Serialize,
+};
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
+pub enum SessionWorkflowNodeKind {
+    Ticket,
+    Action,
+    Decision,
+    Checkpoint,
+    Validation,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
+pub enum SessionWorkflowNodeRequirement {
+    Required,
+    Optional,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
+pub enum SessionWorkflowNodeStatus {
+    Pending,
+    InProgress,
+    Blocked,
+    Done,
+    Deferred,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
+pub enum SessionWorkflowEdgeKind {
+    DependsOn,
+    Order,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionWorkflowNode {
+    pub node_id: String,
+    pub kind: SessionWorkflowNodeKind,
+    pub requirement: SessionWorkflowNodeRequirement,
+    pub status: SessionWorkflowNodeStatus,
+    pub title: String,
+    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
+    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub ticket_urn: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub cached_ticket_title: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub deferred_reason: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub validation_spec_id: Option<String>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionWorkflowEdge {
+    pub from: String,
+    pub to: String,
+    pub kind: SessionWorkflowEdgeKind,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionWorkflowGraph {
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub nodes: Vec<SessionWorkflowNode>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub edges: Vec<SessionWorkflowEdge>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionWorkflowNodeDraft {
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub node_id: Option<String>,
+    pub kind: SessionWorkflowNodeKind,
+    pub requirement: SessionWorkflowNodeRequirement,
+    pub title: String,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub ticket_urn: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub cached_ticket_title: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub validation_spec_id: Option<String>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionWorkflowNodeResolution {
+    pub node_id: String,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub live_ticket_state: Option<String>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionWorkflowDiagnostic {
+    pub node_id: String,
+    pub code: String,
+    pub message: String,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionWorkflowSnapshot {
+    pub workflow: SessionWorkflowGraph,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub resolutions: Vec<SessionWorkflowNodeResolution>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub diagnostics: Vec<SessionWorkflowDiagnostic>,
+}
+
+pub trait SessionTicketStateResolver {
+    fn resolve_ticket_state(
+        &self,
+        ticket_urn: &str,
+    ) -> Result<Option<String>, String>;
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionValidationGate {
+    pub validation_spec_id: String,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub required: bool,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub outcome: Option<String>,
+}
*** End Patch

+use chrono::{
+    DateTime,
+    Utc,
+};
+use serde::{
+    Deserialize,
+    Serialize,
+};
+
+use super::{
+    SessionPinnedEntityHeader,
+    SessionValidationGate,
+    SessionWorkflowSnapshot,
+};
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionHandoffRecord {
+    pub handoff_id: String,
+    pub workspace_session_id: String,
+    pub outgoing_run_id: String,
+    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
+    pub resume_command: String,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub pinned_entities: Vec<SessionPinnedEntityHeader>,
+    pub workflow: SessionWorkflowSnapshot,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub validation: Vec<SessionValidationGate>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionHandoffResult {
+    pub record: SessionHandoffRecord,
+    pub record_path: String,
+    pub render: String,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionFinishRecord {
+    pub workspace_session_id: String,
+    pub run_id: String,
+    pub finished_at: DateTime<Utc>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub deferred_optional_node_ids: Vec<String>,
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub validation: Vec<SessionValidationGate>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SessionFinishResult {
+    pub record: SessionFinishRecord,
+    pub already_finished: bool,
+}

turns.content: The model split is green as well. I’m refreshing the canonical audit queue now; the remaining work should be confined to the storage helper and capture-hook binary, with any still-over-limit parent caught before I proceed.

The refreshed audit confirms `peek` and `transcript_feedback` are clear. `model.rs` is down to 433 and needs only its unit test moved out; storage and the capture-hook binary remain the last substantive production splits.Progress update: the canonical default-400 audit now clears both `transcript_feedback` and `peek`. Their behavior remains validated with `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib` passing 99 tests after each extraction.

`model.rs` is now only 33 lines above the threshold; its inline unit test is the next small extraction. The storage helper and capture binary still need their planned nested responsibility splits.
I’m continuing from the refreshed queue, starting with the small remaining `model.rs` reduction, then I’ll work through storage and the capture-hook binary with the same test-and-audit cycle.

turns.content: For storage, the captured-event identity and canonicalization rules are already a distinct concern from persistence and transcript merging. I’m moving that block into `store/helpers/events.rs`, leaving storage focused on file I/O and record merge policy.

turns.content: The storage event logic is now isolated as a sibling module. I’m running the library suite immediately; this checks the include-based module boundary as well as event merge behavior.

turns.content: The storage extraction passes all tests. The capture binary’s argument parsing, hook-stdin decoding, and path normalization are a cohesive input-adapter layer, so I’m moving them into a nested CLI module and leaving capture orchestration in the binary root.
